files.patch:
@@ -475,4 +475,6 @@ - * A body builder may be connected to its {@link #connectedAncestorBody() nearest ancestor} body builder. This connection - * constrains the order in which the connected builders can finish building, ancestors cannot finish before - * their descendants, and determines the reachability of values - * used by appended operations. + * A body builder may be connected to its {@link #connectedAncestorBody() nearest ancestor} body builder. This + * connection constrains the order in which the connected builders can finish building, ancestors cannot finish + * before their descendants, and determines the reachability of + * values used by appended operations. + *

+ * Body builders are not thread-safe. Block builders associated with a body builder are also not thread-safe.

@@ -34,0 +34,3 @@ + *

+ * Code transformer implementations are not required to be thread-safe. Code transformations operate on block builders + * and code contexts that are not thread-safe.

@@ -61,3 +61,2 @@ - * An operation when initially constructed is unattached from any parent block. - * An unattached operation's state and descendant elements are all immutable except for the operation's - * {@link #result result}, {@link #parent parent} block, and {@link #location}, which are all initially {@code null}. + * The process of building an operation starts with construction of an unattached operation. The + * operation-specific state and any descendant elements of the unattached operation are fixed at construction. @@ -65,8 +64,10 @@ - * An unattached operation can be attached to a block by {@link Block.Builder#op(Op) appending} it to a block that is - * being built. Once attached the operation has a permanently non-{@code null} operation {@link #result} that can be - * used as an operand of subsequent constructed operations. - * After the block is built the operation has a permanently non-{@code null} {@link #parent}. Before then, the block - * being built is not observable through this operation and - * any attempt to access the block throws {@link IllegalStateException}. - *

- * An unattached operation can be built as a {@link #isRoot() root} operation. Once built the operation's + * Building then progresses in one of two ways: + *

    + *
  1. + * the unattached operation is attached to a block, as its parent block, by + * {@link Block.Builder#op(Op) appending} it to a block builder for that block. The attached operation has a permanently + * non-{@code null} {@link #result result} that can be used as an operand of subsequent constructed operations. The + * block being built is not observable through this + * operation and any attempt to access the block throws {@link IllegalStateException}. + *
  2. + * the unattached operation is built as a {@link #isRoot() root} operation. The root operation's @@ -74,0 +75,4 @@ + *
+ *

+ * Building finishes when the parent body builder of the block to which the operation is attached + * finishes, or when the operation is built as a root operation. @@ -80,0 +85,3 @@ + *

+ * An unattached operation, or an operation attached to a block whose parent body builder has not + * finished, is not thread-safe.

@@ -263,1 +263,0 @@
-///
@@ -474,1 +473,1 @@
-/// ## <a id="transforming-heading"/> Transforming
+/// ## <a id="transforming-heading"/>Transforming
@@ -598,0 +597,6 @@
+/// ## Thread safety
+///
+/// The processes of building and transforming code models are not thread-safe. Unless otherwise specified, objects used
+/// to build and transform code models are not thread-safe. Built code models are immutable and may be safely queried
+/// concurrently by multiple threads.
+///
